query: Tengo como 100 memorias en ~/.claude/memory/ (de la version vieja). Como las paso al sistema nuevo?
expected_behavior: Routes to the /antares-memory:migrate command (and architecture.md for the slug layout)
Names /antares-memory:migrate as the tool — it auto-detects ~/.claude/memory/ as a legacy source
States the default mode is dry-run: shows what will move without acting, --apply to commit
Notes the migrator skips files whose target name already exists in the HOME slug (no overwrite)
Notes the indexer auto-runs after migration and the daemon picks up the new files
Notes this is only for the legacy single-path layout — v0.2+ fresh installs need no migration
notes: Guards against: suggesting a manual `mv` instead of migrate.sh; omitting the dry-run vs --apply distinction; and recommending a destination other than the HOME slug's memory/ dir.
